title: GETRANGE
description: Returns a substring of the string stored at a key.
complexity: O(N) where N is the length of the returned string. The complexity is ultimately determined by the returned length, but because creating a substring from an existing string is very cheap, it can be considered O(1) for small strings.

redis_py.signature: getrange(key: KeyT, start: int, end: int)

jedis.signature: String getrange(final String key, final long startOffset, final long endOffset)

content:
Returns the substring of the string value stored at `key`, determined by the offsets `start` and `end` (both are inclusive). Negative offsets can be used in order to provide an offset starting from the end of the string. So -1 means the last character, -2 the penultimate and so forth. The function handles out of range requests by limiting the resulting range to the actual length of the string. ## Examples {{% redis-cli %}} SET mykey "This is a string" GETRANGE mykey 0 3 GETRANGE mykey -3 -1 GETRANGE mykey 0 -1 GETRANGE mykey 10 100 {{% /redis-cli %}} ## Redis Software and Redis Cloud compatibility | Redis
Software | Redis
Cloud | Notes | |:----------------------|:-----------------|:------| | ✅ Standard
✅ Active-Active | ✅ Standard
✅ Active-Active | | ## Return information {{< multitabs id="getrange-return-info" tab1="RESP2" tab2="RESP3" >}} [Bulk string reply](../../develop/reference/protocol-spec#bulk-strings): The substring of the string value stored at key, determined by the offsets start and end (both are inclusive). -tab-sep- [Bulk string reply](../../develop/reference/protocol-spec#bulk-strings): The substring of the string value stored at key, determined by the offsets start and end (both are inclusive). {{< /multitabs >}}
example:
SET mykey "This is a string"
GETRANGE mykey 0 3
GETRANGE mykey -3 -1
GETRANGE mykey 0 -1
GETRANGE mykey 10 100
intro:
Returns the substring of the string value stored at `key`, determined by the
offsets `start` and `end` (both are inclusive).
Negative offsets can be used in order to provide an offset starting from the end
of the string.
So -1 means the last character, -2 the penultimate and so forth.

The function handles out of range requests by limiting the resulting range to
the actual length of the string.

sections.title: Overview
sections.content:
Returns the substring of the string value stored at `key`, determined by the
offsets `start` and `end` (both are inclusive).
Negative offsets can be used in order to provide an offset starting from the end
of the string.
So -1 means the last character, -2 the penultimate and so forth.

The function handles out of range requests by limiting the resulting range to
the actual length of the string.

sections.title: Examples
sections.content:
{{% redis-cli %}}
SET mykey "This is a string"
GETRANGE mykey 0 3
GETRANGE mykey -3 -1
GETRANGE mykey 0 -1
GETRANGE mykey 10 100
{{% /redis-cli %}}
